author: jwhitlock
text: GitHub pull requests with with null for head.repo fail to process

author: jwhitlock
text:
**Describe the bug**

When processing a GitHub pull request event, sometimes the `head.repo` object is `null`:

[taskcluster/services/github/src/api.js](https://github.com/taskcluster/taskcluster/blob/27783611cc09478b99668abc688a1a5b34298a11/services/github/src/api.js#L26)

Line 26
in
[2778361](/taskcluster/taskcluster/commit/27783611cc09478b99668abc688a1a5b34298a11)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | 'event.head.repo.name': eventData.pull\_request.head.repo.name, |

This causes event processing to fail with `Cannot read property 'name' of null`

There is not enough debugging info to determine what event caused the error. The breadcrumbs in sentry may provide a clue.

**To Reproduce**

I do not know how to reproduce this.

**Taskcluster version**

According to Sentry, this was first seen in community-tc in version 44.0.0, and is present in 44.2.2 (latest), seen 5 times in 2 months:

<https://sentry.prod.mozaws.net/share/issue/f378a736276442a7afba00962daa5>

A similar version is marked resolved due to inactivity in firefox-ci, from 37.3.0 to 43.2.0, and seen 23 times over a year:

<https://sentry.prod.mozaws.net/share/issue/a514712054ee477b9a06daeb35166470/>

**Additional context**

Issue [#3455](https://github.com/taskcluster/taskcluster/issues/3455) mentions other missing data items from GitHub APIs.
